front: Define an equivalence relation on a set.
back: An equivalence relation on a set A is a relation that is reflexive (a ~ a for all a ∈ A), symmetric (if a ~ b, then b ~ a), and transitive (if a ~ b and b ~ c, then a ~ c).

front: What is an equivalence class of an element a under an equivalence relation ~?
back: The equivalence class of an element a ∈ A is the set [a] = { b ∈ A | b ~ a }. It contains all elements of A that are related to a.

front: What is the fundamental connection between equivalence relations and partitions?
back: Every equivalence relation on a set A induces a partition of A (the set of all equivalence classes). Conversely, every partition of A induces an equivalence relation (where elements are related if they belong to the same partition block).

front: What is the quotient set of A modulo an equivalence relation ~?
back: The quotient set, denoted A/~, is the set of all equivalence classes of A under the relation ~. It is the partition of A induced by the equivalence relation.

front: State the properties that the equivalence classes of a relation must satisfy to form a partition.
back: For the equivalence classes of a relation ~ on set A to form a partition: 1) Their union must be A. 2) They must be pairwise disjoint (the intersection of any two distinct equivalence classes is empty).
